sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blob moved from (40, 31) to (49, 29) to (54, 28) to (56, 27) and got stuck. G went from 12 to 8 pixels. The blob is at (56, 27) and G is at [[57, 27], [59, 29], [61, 27], [59, 25]] with 8 pixels.
Looking at the image, I can see the blob is very close to G. The G boundary shows it's a diamond shape centered around (59, 27). The blob is at (56, 27) which is just above G.
The blob seems to get stuck when it's adjacent to G but not exactly on it. Maybe I need to click the G pixel itself to absorb/eliminate it.
Let me try clicking the center of G at (59, 27).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blob at (56, 27), G at [[57, 27], [59, 29], [61, 27], [59, 25]] with 8 px
# Try clicking G center to absorb it

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 59, 'col': 27}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
m_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']

print(f"G count: {len(g_nodes)}, G px: {[n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes]}")
print(f"M count: {len(m_nodes)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# If G still exists, try clicking all G boundary pixels
if g_nodes:
    g = g_nodes[0]
    print(f"G boundary: {g['boundary']}")
    for p in g['boundary']:
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': p[0], 'col': p[1]}])
        seg = current_frame.segmentation
        nodes = seg['nodes']
        g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
        print(f"After {p}: G count={len(g_nodes)}, px={[n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes]}")
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
            print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
            break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
